# Screener Weekly — 2026-04-26

*Generated by the Saturday-morning routine. Source data: live `/api/screener/morning/run` against the production Railway deployment.*

## TL;DR

**API failure — no trade data available this week.** Both the screener run endpoint (`POST /api/screener/morning/run`) and the universe endpoint (`GET /api/screener/universe`) returned HTTP 403 with the body `Host not in allowlist`. The Railway deployment has an IP or host allowlist that blocked the claude.ai/code routines execution environment. No ticker data was generated; nothing in this report should be used for Monday trade prep until the underlying API issue is resolved.

## API Failure Details

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Endpoint | `POST /api/screener/morning/run` |
| HTTP Status | 403 |
| Response Body | `Host not in allowlist` |
| Universe Endpoint | `GET /api/screener/universe?mode=swing` |
| Universe HTTP Status | 403 |
| Universe Response Body | `Host not in allowlist` |

## Watch into Monday

*Cannot provide data-driven commentary without a successful screener run. Resolve the Railway host allowlist issue before next Saturday's routine.*

## Caveats / known gaps

- **API blocked (403):** The production Railway deployment at `https://trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app` returned `Host not in allowlist` for both screener endpoints. The claude.ai/code routines runner's outbound IP needs to be added to the Railway deployment's allowlist (or the allowlist restriction needs to be removed/relaxed for authenticated bearer-token requests).
- Plan 1 only ships Coiled Spring — other scans (Pradeep 4%, Qullamaggie EP, Saty Triggers, Vomy, etc.) are deferred to Plan 2.
- `backfill_days_in_compression` is not yet wired into the runner — newly-detected coils start at day 1 even if they've been coiled for weeks. Plan 2 task.
- The compression proxy (rolling stddev/SMA20) is a stand-in for the real Phase Oscillator. Plan 2 will replace it.

## Action required

To fix next Saturday's run, either:
1. Add the outbound IP of the claude.ai/code routines execution environment to the Railway deployment's allowlist, **or**
2. Remove the host allowlist restriction and rely solely on the bearer token for authentication (preferred for a routine runner that may use dynamic IPs).
